g.text: COMMON BUG: Omitting poll_wait → Missed Events / Busy Loop
epoll vs select/poll: How .poll Serves Both
kernel sleeps — waits on registered wqs
Cost: O(1) — pointer store, no sleep
BOTH queues must always be registered
→ new mask is non-zero → return to userspace
BUG A: Forget poll_wait — never woken up
return 0; // mask=0 forever after first call
Result: poll() times out even when writer adds data
BUG B: poll_wait but always return 0 — busy spin
Result: every wake_up re-calls poll, gets 0, loops infinitely
BUG C: Missing POLLOUT — writers starve with full buffer
/* BUG: no poll_wait for write queue */
Result: select(writefds) never returns for this fd
select() / poll() path — O(n) per call
→ mask checked: if non-zero, return to user
→ if all zero: sleep on all registered wqs
→ any wake_up → rescan ALL fds again
Cost: O(n) per event. Bad at n > 1000.
→ poll_wait stores epoll callback in wq
→ fd added to epoll ready list
POLLRDNORM | normal-priority read (= POLLIN for char dev)
POLLWRNORM | normal-priority write (= POLLOUT for char dev)
POLLERR | error — auto-reported, always set on error
Rule: return current state accurately — spurious POLLIN OK
